page: Amazon Web Services c6a.xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with an AMD EPYC 7R13 processor running at 3.6 GHz. The instance provides 4 dedicated vCPUs across 2 physical cores with hyperthreading enabled, translating to 2 threads per core. Memory configuration consists of 8.0 GB of DDR4 RAM operating at 3 GHz, yielding a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio of 2.0 GB per core. There is no local storage or GPU bundled with this instance, and baseline network bandwidth is rated at 2 Gbps.

Benchmark data reveals a contrast between CPU and memory performance. The instance demonstrates strong single-core CPU performance in stress-ng tests, placing in the top 25%. However, multi-core CPU performance and Passmark CPU scores fall into the average category. Memory performance is a notable bottleneck, with memory bandwidth and Passmark memory scores ranking in the weak and poor categories, respectively. Cryptographic capabilities are supported by hardware-accelerated AES, VAES, and SHA_NI instruction sets, resulting in average performance in encryption benchmarks.

The c6a.xlarge offers a cost-efficient entry point for compute-bound workloads that do not require massive memory footprints or high memory bandwidth. Due to the weak memory bandwidth and database operation benchmarks, this instance is less suited for memory-intensive databases or high-throughput web serving. Instead, it is best utilized for single-threaded processing tasks, basic batch processing, lightweight text processing, and small-scale LLM inference text generation where compute density is prioritized over memory speed.
description: Amazon Web Services c6a.xlarge is a compute-optimized instance utilizing the x86_64 architecture and powered by an AMD EPYC 7R13 processor running at 3.6 GHz. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs, 8.0 GB of DDR4 RAM, and a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps, with no bundled local storage or GPU. Performance benchmarks indicate strong single-core CPU capabilities, placing in the top 25% in stress-ng tests, while multi-core performance and cryptographic execution remain average. Conversely, memory bandwidth and memory scores are weak to poor, representing a clear hardware tradeoff. This resource profile makes the instance qualitatively cost-efficient for compute-heavy, memory-light workloads. It is best suited for single-threaded processing, lightweight text processing, and small-scale model inference, rather than memory-intensive database operations or high-throughput web serving.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c6a.xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 4 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 8.0 GB of DDR4 RAM, and strong single-core CPU performance with weak memory bandwidth.

tagline: A compute-focused virtual server featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processors, strong single-core performance, and a balanced, memory-lean architecture.
bullet_points: Powered by a 3.6 GHz AMD EPYC 7R13 processor with 4 dedicated vCPUs.
Equipped with 8.0 GB of DDR4 RAM and 2 Gbps baseline network bandwidth.
Strong single-core CPU performance contrasted by weak memory bandwidth.
Includes hardware-accelerated cryptography via AES, VAES, and SHA_NI.
Best suited for single-threaded compute tasks and lightweight text processing.
